# core.solver.MoERoutingModel { #mlsysim.core.solver.MoERoutingModel }

```python
core.solver.MoERoutingModel()
```

Models first-order Mixture-of-Experts routing imbalance and optional
expert-parallel all-to-all cost.

Sparse models decouple memory from compute: total parameters determine memory,
while active parameters determine the compute path. `MoERoutingModel` adds one
teachable knob, `routing_imbalance_factor`, for hot-expert effects.

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [solve](#mlsysim.core.solver.MoERoutingModel.solve) | Estimate effective active parameters and optional EP all-to-all latency. |

### solve { #mlsysim.core.solver.MoERoutingModel.solve }

```python
core.solver.MoERoutingModel.solve(
    model,
    batch_size,
    seq_len,
    precision='fp16',
    ep_size=1,
    routing_imbalance_factor=1.0,
    fleet=None,
)
```

#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| model | SparseTransformerWorkload | Sparse Transformer / MoE workload. | _required_ |
| batch_size | int | Local routed batch size. | _required_ |
| seq_len | int | Sequence length. | _required_ |
| precision | str | Activation precision for routed bytes. | `'fp16'` |
| ep_size | int | Expert-parallel degree. | `1` |
| routing_imbalance_factor | float | Hot-expert multiplier, where `1.0` is balanced. | `1.0` |
| fleet | Fleet | Optional fabric used to compute all-to-all latency. | `None` |

#### Returns

`MoERoutingResult` with effective active experts, effective active parameters,
routed bytes, and optional all-to-all latency.
